seoTitle: Green Hills vs Green Point — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Green Hills and Green Point. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Green Hills vs Green Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Green Hills and Green Point.
heading: Green Hills vs Green Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Green Hills, New South Wales and Green Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Green Hills has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Green Point (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Green Hills or Green Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Green Hills has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Green Point scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Green Hills scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. Green Point scores 35/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Green Hills or Green Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Green Hills has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
